description: Standard specification for structural steel shapes
notes: Default grade for all W-shapes. Replaced A36 as standard.

typical_use: HSS round shapes, round pipe columns
weldability: Good — may need preheat for thick walls
notes: Standard grade for round HSS. Check wall thickness for weld requirements.

typical_use: HSS rectangular/square shapes, HSS columns and bracing
weldability: Good — may need preheat for thick walls (≥1/2")
notes: Standard grade for rectangular/square HSS. Higher Fy than Gr. B.
Legacy standard. Still used for angles, channels, and plates. Dual-certified with A992 for many W-shapes.

weldability: Good — preheat may be required for thick plates
notes: Used for plates and built-up members. Same Fy as A992.
Less common than Gr. 50. Check availability.

weldability: Excellent — superior to A992 for thick sections, no preheat needed
notes: Premium grade for heavy sections. Better CVN toughness. Typically 5-10% cost premium.

description: HSLA quenched and self-tempered shapes — Grade 65
weldability: Good — follow AWS D1.1 for thick sections
notes: High-strength grade for reducing column sizes. Verify availability and lead time.

description: High-yield-strength quenched and tempered alloy steel plates
typical_use: Base plates, heavy gusset plates, built-up columns
weldability: Requires special procedures — preheat, low-hydrogen electrodes, controlled heat input
notes: Very high strength but difficult to weld. Use only when specified by engineer.

typical_use: Exposed structural steel, bridges, exterior applications
weldability: Good — use matching weathering filler metals
notes: Forms protective patina, no painting required. Not suitable for submerged or constantly wet conditions. Also known as COR-TEN.

typical_use: Standard weight, extra-strong, and double extra-strong pipe
notes: Standard pipe grade. Available in STD, XS (extra strong), and XXS (double extra strong) wall thicknesses.
Most common structural bolt. Superseded by ASTM F3125 Gr. A325 but still commonly referenced. Available as bearing (N/X) or slip-critical (SC).

typical_use: Heavy connections, moment frames, high-load joints
weldability: N/A — bolts not welded, never galvanized
notes: Higher capacity than A325. Cannot be galvanized. Superseded by ASTM F3125 Gr. A490.

description: Twist-off type tension control bolt (TC bolt) — A325 equivalent
typical_use: Field connections — easier installation verification than A325
notes: TC bolts have a splined end that shears off at proper pretension. Same capacity as A325. Commonly used for slip-critical connections.

typical_use: Fillet welds, CJP welds, PJP welds on A36/A992/A572 steel
weldability: N/A — this IS the filler metal
notes: Standard electrode classification. FEXX = 70 ksi. Matches A992 and lower steels. Common designations: E7018 (SMAW), ER70S-6 (GMAW), E71T-1 (FCAW).
